title: neurolepis stuebelii
intro: Neurolepis stuebelii, also known as Chilco or Queñua, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Andean region and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating digestive issues. Neurolepis stuebelii is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, which can be taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
